title: Senior Sales Specialist (e-Commerce)
description:
Step into the spotlight with Shure!

We’re looking for a Senior Sales Specialist for European Strategic Direct Accounts (ESDA) to help drive success with our key international and online reselling partners.

In this dynamic role, you’ll manage daily operations, support exciting projects, and handle unique customer requests, all while keeping communication sharp and processes smooth across the board.

You’ll report to the Senior Sales Manager ESDA, based in London (UK) or Eppingen (Germany), depending on your location.

Ready to make some noise in the world of strategic sales?

Apply now and help us shape the future of sound with Europe’s most influential accounts!

Responsibilities

Manage E-commerce Operations:


 * Take effective care of all our online shop operations – including e. g. inventory management, timely fill-ups, end-customer cases, and key metrics checks.
 * Collaborate with internal departments, such as customer service, marketing, finance and IT, as well as our external partner.
 * Work on and steer cross-functional tasks and projects.
 * Own/Attend relevant meetings.
   
   

Optimize Online Sales


 * Support the development and execution of online sales campaigns and digital marketing campaigns.
 * Focus on customer experience and usability.
 * Remain up to date on industry trends and best practices in e-commerce, in marketplace/online sales.
 * Generate ideas and check options to improve the online shop and processes continuously.
 * Collect customer feedback and analyse customer behaviour.
 * Check and update product listings regularly.
   
   

Team Efforts


 * Support our E-Commerce strategy and execution in Western Europe.
 * Support operations with our key online reseller accounts.
 * Bring in your very best to support and actively influence the positive development of ESDA team and its key accounts.
   
   

Qualifications


 * 5 years’ experience in e-commerce or online/marketplace sales.
 * Degree in Business, Economics, Sales or related field
 * Fluency in English. German as an additional language is an advantage.
 * Strong self-organizational and multitasking abilities
 * Proactive, pragmatic problem-solving skills
 * Demonstratable experience meeting deadlines
 * Team player skilled in collaborating with cross-functional internal and external teams to achieve optimal outcomes.
 * Willingness, strive and discipline to grow
 * Excellent, cross-cultural, verbal and written communication skills; ability to communicate effectively and appropriately with team members, partners and customers.
 * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Outlook, Teams, Word, PowerPoint); ideally you have some experience with Salesforce, Microsoft Loop and perhaps SAP.
 * Ability to travel a few times per year.
   
   

Who We Are

Shure’s mission is to be the most trusted audio brand worldwide – and for over a century, our Core Values have aligned us to be just that. Founded in 1925, we are a leading global manufacturer of audio equipment known for quality, reliability, and durability. We engineer microphones, headphones, wireless audio systems, conferencing systems, and more. And quality doesn’t stop at our products. Our talented teams strive for perfection and innovate every chance they get. We offer an Associate-first culture, flexible work arrangements, and opportunity for all.

Shure is headquartered in United States. We have more than 35 regional sales offices, engineering hubs, distribution centers and manufacturing facilities throughout the Americas, EMEA, and Asia.

THE MIX MATTERS

Don’t check off every box in the job requirements? No problem! We recognize that every professional journey is unique and are committed to providing an equitable candidate experience for all prospective Shure Associates. If you’re excited about this role, believe you’ve got the skills to be successful, and share our passion for creating an inclusive, diverse, equitable, and accessible work environment, then apply!

title: Sales Specialist - Retail (m/f/d) – in Vollzeit
description:
Wer Wir Sind

Als eine der weltweit führenden Schmierstoffmarken kann Castrol auf eine lange Tradition der Innovation und Unterstützung von Wegbereitenden zurückblicken. Dank unserer Leidenschaft für Höchstleistung und einer schon immer stark auf Kooperation und Partnerschaft ausgerichteten Philosophie konnte Castrol Schmierstoffe und Fette entwickeln, die seit mehr als 125 Jahren für die Mobilität an Land, auf dem Wasser, in der Luft und im Weltraum führend sind.

Castrol ist Teil von bp und bietet Produkte und Dienstleistungen für Kund: innen und Verbraucher: innen in den Branchen Automobil, Marine, Industrie und Energie. Unsere Markenprodukte stehen weltweit für Innovation, Technologieführerschaft, Leistungsstärke und ein kompromissloses Bekenntnis zur Qualität.

Wie Du Die Zukunft Mitgestalten Kannst

Als Sales Specialist arbeitest du in einem internationalen Team (Retail Europe – Central, Ost) und bist verantwortlich für die Umsetzung des Vertriebsplans sowie den Ausbau des Schmierstoffgeschäfts in den Segmenten Einzelhandel, E-Commerce und PSS (Tankstellen). Dabei betreust du bestehende Partner und gewinnst neue Kunden im zugewiesenen Vertriebsgebiet.

Dein Neues Aufgabenumfeld – Sicher, Anspruchsvoll Und Zukunftsorientiert


 * Umsetzung des regionalen Verkaufsplans zur Erreichung der Vertriebsziele und Maximierung des Wachstums.
 * Betreuung und Ausbau des bestehenden Kundenportfolios, insbesondere im Einzelhandel und E-Commerce
 * Entwicklung und Umsetzung von Verkaufsstrategien zur Kundengewinnung und Markenstärkung von Castrol
 * Zusammenarbeit mit den relevanten Marketingabteilungen zur Planung gemeinsamer Marketingkampagnen. Verwaltung des Marketingbudgets und Sicherstellung der Umsetzung gemäß Plan
 * Organisation von Verkaufsaktionen und Anreizprogrammen für Einzelhandelskunden
 * Analyse von Verkaufsdaten und Markttrends zur Identifikation von Chancen und Risiken
 * Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Qualitäts-, Marken- und Sicherheitsstandards bei Kunden
 * Effiziente Nutzung digitaler Tools wie Salesforce, Power BI und Tableau zur Kunden- und Gebietsverwaltung
   
   

Dein Qualifikationsprofil – Fundiert Und Aus Leidenschaft


 * Abgeschlossenes Bachelorstudium in einem für die Stelle relevanten Fachbereich
 * Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Einzelhandel (B2C), E-Commerce oder Tankstellengeschäft
 * Erfahrung im Kunden- und Channel-Management
 * Kenntnisse im funktionsübergreifenden Projektmanagement und in mehrschichtigen Organisationsstrukturen
 * Erfahrung in der Umsetzung von Marketingprogrammen und im Angebotsmanagement
 * Produkt- und Dienstleistungskenntnisse sowie Kundensegmentierung
 * Kenntnisse der strategischen Account-Entwicklung und Kundenaktivierung
 * Sicheres Kundenbeziehungsmanagement und Verständnis von Kundenprofitabilität
 * Kenntnisse in Vertragswesen und Abschlussprozessen
 * Gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (mind. B2-Niveau)
   
   

Work-Life Balance und was sonst noch für uns spricht:


 * Willkommen an Bord: Onboarding leicht gemacht.
 * Flexibel Arbeiten: Flexible Modelle, freie Tage und Sabbaticals inklusive.
 * Top Vergütung: Gehalt, Boni und zahlreiche Benefits.
 * Entfalte Dich!: Tausende Möglichkeiten zur Weiterbildung und persönlichem Wachstum.
 * Fit bleiben: Gesundheit & Wohlfühlen im Fokus.
 * Team Spirit: Events und gemeinsamer Spaß.
 * Doppelt Gutes tun: Spenden werden verdoppelt.
   
   

Für alle Details rund um das Thema Benefits besuche unsere Website (Arbeiten bei bp | Deine Karriere bei bp | bp in Deutschland)

Wir engagieren uns für Gleichstellung, Diversität sowie die Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Familie. Menschen mit einer Schwerbehinderung werden bei entsprechender Eignung vorrangig eingestellt.

title: Vertriebsspezialist
description:
Deine Mission




Wir suchen einen echten Hunter im Vertrieb, der Freude daran hat, neue Kunden zu gewinnen, Beziehungen aufzubauen und Abschlüsse erfolgreich ins Ziel zu bringen.




Als Vertriebsexperte / Sales Specialist (m/w/d) bist du verantwortlich für:




 * Neukundenakquise: Du jagst aktiv neue Geschäftsmöglichkeiten, kontaktierst Entscheider in Fashion, Apparel und E-Commerce und baust unser Kundennetzwerk aus.
 * Reaktivierung & Upselling: Du belebst inaktive Kundenbeziehungen und entdeckst neue Potenziale.
 * Abschlussorientierung: Mit überzeugenden Präsentationen und smarten Verhandlungen bringst du Deals sicher zum Abschluss.
 * Marktpräsenz: Du vertrittst Humanetics auf Branchenveranstaltungen und Messen, stärkst unsere Sichtbarkeit und baust dein Netzwerk aus.
 * Teamwork: Gemeinsam mit Marketing, Consulting und Development entwickelst du Lösungen, die Kunden begeistern.




Dein Profil




 * Du bist ein Vertriebsprofi mit Biss und Leidenschaft für Akquise? Dann passt du perfekt!
 * Hunter-Mentalität: Hohe Eigenmotivation, Freude am Jagen neuer Kunden und Abschlussorientierung.
 * Kommunikationsstärke: Schlagfertig, überzeugend und präsentationssicher.
 * Qualifikation: Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Vertrieb, Marketing, Wirtschaft, Ingenieurwesen oder vergleichbare Ausbildung.
 * Sprachen: Deutsch und Englisch (C1) – weitere europäische Sprachen sind ein Plus.
 * Bonus: Vertriebserfahrung in Fashion/Apparel, Tech, Gaming oder digitalen Lösungen bzw. Body-Data-/Body-Scanning-Technologien.




Das bieten wir dir




 * Gestaltungsfreiraum: Übernimm Verantwortung für deinen Markt und gestalte aktiv die digitale Transformation der Modebranche mit.
 * Flexibilität: 30 Tage Urlaub, flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit zum mobilen Arbeiten.
 * Benefits: JobRad-Programm, betriebliche Altersvorsorge, kostenfreie Parkplätze, kostenlose Getränke.
 * Kultur: Offene Kommunikation, flache Hierarchien, echte Teamarbeit – und regelmäßige Mitarbeiterevents, bei denen auch der Spaß nicht zu kurz kommt.




👉 Klingt nach deinem nächsten Schritt?




Dann werde Teil unseres Teams und revolutioniere mit uns nicht nur die Modebranche, sondern auch andere Technologiebranchen – von Tech-Giganten über Gaming bis hin zu Humanoids – durch Body Data & Sizing Solutions!

title: Vertriebsexperte / Sales Specialist (m/w/d) Fashion & Apparel | Body Data & Sizing Solutions
description:
Humanetics verfolgt das Ziel, Produktdesign für alle Menschen zugänglich zu machen – indem wir den Menschen konsequent in den Mittelpunkt der Produktentwicklung stellen. Mit modernster 3D- und 4D-Bodyscanning-Technologie, der weltweit größten anthropometrischen Datenbank und führenden Softwarelösungen wie iSize, Anthroscan und RAMSIS unterstützen wir Unternehmen dabei, Produkte zu entwickeln, die die tatsächlichen Bedürfnisse der Nutzer erfüllen.

Unsere Body-Data-Expertise kommt in Branchen wie Automobil, Mode, Gesundheitswesen und Sicherheit zum Einsatz – überall dort, wo es um Komfort, Funktionalität und Sicherheit geht.

Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams in Kaiserslautern suchen wir in Vollzeit (40 Stunden/Woche) ab sofort einen


Vertriebsexperten / Sales Specialist (m/w/d) Fashion & Apparel | Body Data & Sizing Solutions

Deine Mission:

Wir suchen einen echten Hunter (m/w/d) im Vertrieb, der Freude daran hat, neue Kunden zu gewinnen, Beziehungen aufzubauen und Abschlüsse erfolgreich ins Ziel zu bringen.


Als Sales Specialist (m/w/d) bist du verantwortlich für:

 * Neukundenakquise: Du gewinnst aktiv neue Geschäftsmöglichkeiten, kontaktierst Entscheider in Fashion, Apparel und E-Commerce und baust unser Kundennetzwerk aus
 * Reaktivierung & Upselling: Du belebst inaktive Kundenbeziehungen und entdeckst neue Potenziale
 * Abschlussorientierung: Mit überzeugenden Präsentationen und smarten Verhandlungen bringst du Deals sicher zum Abschluss
 * Marktpräsenz: Du vertrittst Humanetics auf Branchenveranstaltungen und Messen, stärkst unsere Sichtbarkeit und baust dein Netzwerk aus
 * Teamwork: Gemeinsam mit Marketing, Consulting und Development entwickelst du Lösungen, die Kunden begeistern


Dein Profil:

Du bist ein Vertriebsprofi mit Biss und Leidenschaft für Akquise? Dann passt du perfekt!

 * Hunter-Mentalität: hohe Eigenmotivation, Freude am Gewinnen neuer Kunden und Abschlussorientierung
 * Kommunikationsstärke: schlagfertig, überzeugend und präsentationssicher
 * Qualifikation: abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Vertrieb, Marketing, Wirtschaft, Ingenieurwesen oder vergleichbare Ausbildung
 * Sprachen: Deutsch und Englisch (C1) – weitere europäische Sprachen sind ein Plus
 * Bonus: Vertriebserfahrung in Fashion/Apparel, digitalen Lösungen oder Body-Data-/Body-Scanning-Technologien


Das bieten wir dir:

 * Gestaltungsfreiraum: Übernimm Verantwortung für deinen Markt und gestalte aktiv die digitale Transformation der Modebranche mit
 * Flexibilität: 30 Tage Urlaub, flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit zum mobilen Arbeiten
 * Benefits: JobRad-Programm, betriebliche Altersvorsorge, kostenfreie Parkplätze, kostenlose Getränke
 * Kultur: offene Kommunikation, flache Hierarchien, echte Teamarbeit – und regelmäßige Mitarbeiterevents, bei denen auch der Spaß nicht zu kurz kommt


Klingt nach deinem nächsten Abenteuer?

Dann werde Teil unseres Teams und arbeite mit uns an der Mission, mit Body Data und Sizing Solutions die Modebranche zu revolutionieren!


Konnten wir dein Interesse wecken? Dann freuen wir uns darauf, dich bald kennenzulernen. Bitte bewirb dich vorzugsweise online über den folgenden Link.


Für Rückfragen steht dir Frau Claudia Karl gerne per E-Mail unter hr.hdeu@humaneticsgroup.com zur Verfügung.


Unsere Datenschutzhinweise kannst du unter https://www.human-solutions.com/de/footer/datenschutz.html einsehen. Bei Humanetics ist der Datenschutz und die Informationssicherheit ein wichtiges Merkmal unserer Unternehmenskultur und liegt in der Verantwortung aller Mitarbeitenden. Humanetics ist zudem ein Arbeitgeber, der die Chancengleichheit fördert – unabhängig von Geschlecht, Herkunft, sexueller Orientierung oder einer Behinderung.


Humanetics Digital Europe GmbH | Personalabteilung | Europaallee 10 | 67657 Kaiserslautern

ax140132by

title: Sales Director, E-Commerce Channel D-A-CH (m/f/d) – fixed-term contract
description:
Job Description

Are you an experienced leader, ready to head up our E-Commerce channel and craft future E-Commerce B2B Sales in the EUC Business Unit (D-A-CH) for the LEGO Group?

We are looking for a strong personality, with energy and drive, great leadership skills and a clear vision of how to impact the sales agenda in D-A-CH and beyond. You will be an important voice of the Europe Central (EUC) Leadership Team as well as the Sales Leadership Team and will have responsibility for building and maintaining partnerships and creating joint value to drive business growth!

Please note This position is on a fixed-term contract basis until end of December 2026 and will be based in our LEGO® Sales Office in Grasbrunn, close to Munich and will follow a hybrid working model with 3 days in the office and up to 2 days remotely. Relocation support is not offered.

The closing date for applications will be Friday 31st October 2025.


Core Responsibilities


   
   
   
   
 * Continue to develop the LEGO® business in the B2B E-Commerce channel in a strategic and sustainable way. Drive success with the top strategic customers.
 * Develop and implement the sales strategy for E-Commerce ensuring the business delivers against sales and profitability objectives.
 * Provide channel inputs into the Business Unit's annual business planning and channel strategy planning.
 * Lead, empower and develop a team of 12 professionals covering different levels of scope and areas of responsibility.
 * Upskill and develop the overall competence and professional skills within the E-Com sales function & drive innovation within and beyond the team by scouting business-changing trends and working with partners to actively impact change in the retail landscape.
 * Drive customer value proposition, engage with key customers, and develop strong relationships.
 * Leading strategic joint business agenda with customers, developing annual business plans and managing in-year negotiations and course correction plans.
 * Lead integration between key e-retailers, partners (vendor), and internal stakeholders driving effective and constructive collaboration.
   
   
   
   

Play your part in our team succeeding

The Europe Central (D-A-CH) Sales Team works in 3 Retail Channels (Toy Specialist, Grocery & Department Stores, E-Commerce) complemented by Customer Development and Field Sales plus Field Sales Management. The Key Account Management Team within EUC Sales is responsible for successfully delivering the Customer Value Proposition to our key partners in order to fulfil our Partner Promise of Mutual Value Creation and generate sustainable growth. The role is responsible for the E-Commerce Channel (Pure Players and Omnichannel Players with a majority of Online Sales) and reports into the General Manager Europe Central!

Do you have what it takes?


   
   
   
   
 * Extensive experience in commercial front-line roles out of which some in E-Commerce, P&L management experience and proven negotiation skills
 * Proven experience managing a complex B2B eCommerce retail landscape, with a strong understanding of key dependencies
 * Skilled in developing tailored, high-impact strategies for individual accounts to drive sustainable growth and ensure long-term success
 * Proven team leadership experience and success in developing people and digital savviness
 * Extensive experience in driving commercial planning and management, value-based selling, mutual revenue and profit growth, consistent brand execution and joint business planning
 * Strong Digital Marketing and/or technology experience
 * Experience working with E-Commerce platforms and marketplaces as well as experience implementing technology solutions & ecosystems
 * Strong analytical skills
 * Fluent German and English language skills
   
   
   
   

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

title: Junior Account Manager (m/f/d)
description:
Job Description

We are looking for a commercially minded and energized Associate Account Manager to successfully help us to deliver outstanding results which contribute to both our partners and customers as well as the LEGO Group.

If you are passionate to grow the LEGO Group’s business in Europe Central (DACH), then this is the role for you. Bring your learning mentality and your entrepreneurial spirit and become part of our Toy Specialist Channel team!

Please note This position will be based in our LEGO® Sales Office in Grasbrunn, close to Munich and will follow a hybrid working model with 3 days in the office and up to 2 days remotely. Relocation support is not offered.

The closing date for applications will be Friday 24th October 2025.


Core Responsibilities


   
   
   
   
 * Co-responsibility for one of the largest omnichannel retail customers Manage relationship and business operations for the top European toy specialist customer within the DACH - region alongside the Senior KAM as well as the Account Manager and thereby achieve our strategic business goals. As a key go-to-person for the team on customer side you will bring the LEGO® brand to live in brick & mortar stores as well as online.
 * Contribute to commercial planning, collaborate closely with our eCommerce, Trade Marketing and Field sales team and ensure the state-of-the-art execution of all planned activities. This will position you in the driver’s seat for the start of your own career within LEGO® EUC’s Key Account Management team.
 * Support of Senior Key Account Manager & collaborator management Provide excellent support to the Sr. KAM and get on the job coaching from an experienced Sales Manager. You will act closely as a triangle team (Sr. KAM, Account Manager, Junior Account Manager) and strive for achieving the targets set out in your account plans. In addition, you will be collaborating with sales colleagues in other European LEGO® business units working on the same customer.
 * Be an active part of the planning & execution process of the yearly Sales & Marketing calendar Your toolbox will reach from launch and occasion based campaigns to engaging consumer & shopper content as well as sales driving promotions. Plan, implement, keep track as well as follow-up on these topics jointly with the Senior Key Account Manager.
 * Consistent Brand Execution Collaborate across the LEGO® Group to ensure the seamless shopper experience is consistent across all channels and geography in delivering the LEGO® brand values. Use cross-functional insights and analytics to develop multi-channel shopper strategies that achieve excellent execution with consistency at all points of purchase.
 * Grow Active Consumer Base All activations & projects with the retailer are fueling into the goal of actively increasing the LEGO® Group’s active consumer base and thereby reaching more kids for us as a brand.
   
   
   
   

Play your part in our team succeeding!

The Europe Central (D-A-CH) Sales Team works in 3 Retail Channels (Toy Specialist, Grocery & Department Stores, E-Commerce) complemented by Customer Development and Field Sales plus Field Sales Management. The Key Account Management Team within EUC Sales is responsible for successfully delivering the Customer Value Proposition to our key partners in order to fulfil our Partner Promise of Mutual Value Creation and generate sustainable growth.

Do You Have What It Takes


   
   
   
   
 * First working experiences in Commercial functions and preferably first experiences in Account management (ideally within Consumer Goods)
 * First experience in omnichannel activations on Marketing and/or Sales side
 * You are energized if you can get things done, demonstrate positive energy and you are ambitious to develop your sales talent
 * You enjoy working analytically and get energy from articulating conclusions and recommendations out of your analysis
 * Organizational talent and ability to prioritize the important from the less important tasks
 * Profound knowledge of MS-Office, especially Excel
 * Proficient in German and English
   
   
   
   

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

title: Implementation Specialist (DACH)
description:
We're a global all-in-one e-commerce SaaS platform that helps online sellers manage their operations across multiple sales channels. From order management, inventory syncing, and shipping integrations, to full marketplace automation, Base.com is trusted by thousands of e-commerce businesses around the world to scale and optimize their operations.

With deep integrations into Amazon, eBay, Shopify, WooCommerce, DHL, FedEx, and dozens more, we empower merchants to simplify operations and grow faster. We've grown 15x in the past 3 years, with teams in Europe, the Americas, and Asia—and we're now growing in Germany, Switzerland and Austria.

We are looking for an Implementation Specialist with experience in SaaS onboarding, merchant integrations, or technical customer success to support our growth. This is a great opportunity for someone who has previously worked as a Solutions Consultant, Sales Engineer, or Onboarding Specialist in a tech startup, e-commerce company, or ERP/SaaS provider.

In this role, you will lead technical onboarding projects, helping new customers configure their Base.com account, integrate with their online stores and marketplaces, and get value from day one. You'll act as the trusted advisor throughout the implementation journey and ensure a smooth go-live experience for each customer.

Responsibilities


 * Own and manage end-to-end implementation projects for e-commerce clients in Germany, Switzerland and Austria
 * Conduct discovery calls to map client requirements, processes, and goals
 * Lead product walkthroughs, platform configuration, and API integrations with ERPs, shipping carriers, and marketplaces
 * Provide technical product training and onboarding support to merchants
 * Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, Product, Onboarding and Support teams to ensure customer satisfaction
 * Troubleshoot integration issues and coordinate with developers when needed
 * Develop reusable implementation templates, guides, and help center articles
 * Contribute to customer success by driving adoption and helping clients optimize Base.com usage.
   
   

Requirements


 * 3+ years of experience in e-commerce implementation, SaaS onboarding, pre-sales, or solutions consulting
 * Experience with ERP integration, order processing systems, or retail operations
 * Understanding of e-commerce workflows, logistics, catalog and inventory management
 * Strong project management and stakeholder communication skills
 * Fluent in German and proficient in English
 * Comfort working with APIs, platform configuration tools, or basic scripting (e.g. PHP, JavaScript, )
 * Strong problem-solving mindset - independently analyze situations, finding solutions, workarounds and non-standard solutions when needed
   
   

Nice if you Have


 * Experience working as a Sales Engineer, Customer Success Engineer, or Technical Account Manager
 * Familiarity with e-commerce automation, shipping software, POS systems, or CRM tools
 * Has managed an e-shop before
 * Startup experience and comfort working in fast-paced environments.
   
   

Benefits


 * Join a global, fast-scaling e-commerce SaaS company with a leading tech product
 * Be a key player in Base.com's Germany, Switzerland and Austria market expansion
 * Competitive compensation and high autonomy
 * Hybrid work model with flexibility
 * Collaborate with international teams and work on high-impact projects
 * Permanent employment contract
 * 30 vacation days per year and 24.12. as well as 31.12. off

title: Warehouse Logistics Manager
description:
Leon Cycle is an e-bike business that was established in Germany in 2014 and has since developed business operations in over 10 countries across Europe, North America and APAC. Leon Cycle offers a range of e-bike models from mountain bikes, folding e-bikes, trekking e-bikes and step thru e-bikes. Our range of products are sold via both our own online website as well as E-Commerce platforms and via our dealers and our own retail stores worldwide.




With strong growth and expansions in place, we are now seeking sales professionals to join us as Warehouse Logistics Manager in Hanover. The role will include but not limited to:




 * Management and leadership of Leon Cycle warehouse and logistics operations in DACH.
 * Business partner with BD Managers to ensure all supply chain, domestic logistical issues and warehouse operations is running efficiently to support business sales growth
 * Business partner with factories in RO/CZ/FR/CN to be up to date on all incoming stocks and inventory, ensure full utilization of warehouse space and ensure accurate inventory count, lead a team of warehouse/logistics specialist if needed
 * Manage all inventory returns, escalate any critical returns/faults/product safety issues on a weekly and monthly report to ops director.
 * Manage any other adhoc operations duties related to Logistics & Warehouse when needed.




The ideal candidate should exhibit:




 * 5-8 years of Logistics & Warehouse management and support experience
 * Previously worked for a retail / trading and/or E-Commerce business
 * Strong leader mindset but with hands-on ability to also handle daily execution if needed
 * Has worked in a small to mid size business before who can multi-task
sector: Sporting Goods Manufacturing, Retail, and Manufacturing

title: E-Commerce Catalog Specialist (m/w/d)
description:
DocMorris stands for customer-centred, innovative services and a wide range of digital healthcare products. These range from quickly making appointments to see a doctor online on the TeleClinic telemedicine platform to pharmaceutical advice and the supply of prescription and over-the-counter medicines and healthcare products from Germany's best-known online pharmacy.

In addition, there is the marketplace with a broad complementary range of remedies and aids as well as products from the areas of nutrition, beauty and family. DocMorris thus provides its customers with easy access to comprehensive healthcare services in one place with just one click. In the Germany segment, the Swiss company DocMorris AG generated external sales of CHF 1,022 million in 2024 with more than 10 million active customers.




Join Our Marketplace Team as a Catalog Specialist and Shape the Future of E-commerce!

Are you detail-oriented and proactive?

Do you have a knack for making product information not only accurate but also visually captivating?

If so, we want you to join our Marketplace team at DocMorris as a Catalog Specialist.

Here's your chance to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our product catalogue and ensuring our customers have the best shopping experience!




Your Role:

As a Catalog Specialist, you will be the backbone of our product listings. You'll ensure every product description, specification, and image is meticulously crafted, up-to-date, and optimised for search and sales performance. Creating new SKU’s and refining existing ones will be your daily mission, always aiming for the highest standard of visual appeal and accuracy.




Tasks

 * Create and Update SKU’s: Develop and manage product listings with detailed descriptions, specs, and high-quality images. Regularly refresh and optimise product content to stay ahead of market trends and ensure top-notch visual appeal.
 * Catalog Feature Development: Help defining the roadmap for new catalogue features, focusing on enhancing product pages.
 * Quality Control: Conduct thorough audits to maintain consistency and quality across all product listings. Establish and uphold guidelines and standards to ensure professionalism.
 * Collaboration and Communication: Work closely with marketing, sales, and customer service teams to support product launches and promotions.
 * Reporting and OKRs: Report track catalog performance towards results.




Strengths

 * A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Information Management, or a related field.
 * Proven experience in catalog management, preferably in e-commerce or retail.
 * Exceptional attention to detail and organisational skills.
 * Excellent written and verbal communication abilities.
 * Proficiency in using catalog management systems and e-commerce platforms.
 * Ability to thrive both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
 * Knowledge of beauty and personal care products is a plus.
 * Fluency in German and strong English language skills is a must




Note: We are unable to offer work visas for this position.




Our Benefits

 * Company pension scheme with attractive employer contributions
 * 30 days of holiday
 * Opportunity to participate in the employee participation program (stock options)
 * Commuting cost reimbursement
 * Training opportunities and regular development meetings
 * Access to eLearning platforms such as LinkedIn Learning
 * Shopping discounts through Corporate Benefits
 * Great employee events and annual company celebrations
 * Feel good working environment with air-conditioned, bright offices and ergonomically equipped workstations with modern technical equipment
 * Flexible working hours and home office options
